summary: sensivity ships a Windows game-cheat with disguised OneDrive persistence, browser URL surveillance, and obfuscated license phone-home
details: On require, launcher.js (declared as `main`) spawns a detached supervisor/worker daemon that respawns itself, terminates any process holding TCP 3000 (`Stop-Process -Id $_.OwningProcess -Force`), and installs HKCU Run persistence under the deceptive name `OneDriveUpdate` pointing at `OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs` in the package directory — re-launching the package on every Windows logon. Every 3 seconds, launcher.js shells out to PowerShell + UIAutomation to enumerate Edit controls inside chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave windows and read the URL bar of every tab in those browsers (cross-process surveillance of the installer's browsing). launcher.js then `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js','utf8'))`s a 161 KB obfuscator.io-style bundle (RC4 string array, control-flow flattening) which `require`s `sens.node` — a 6.8 MB unsigned Windows PE whose extracted strings (`Enable FL9`, `Freecam`, `Teleport`, `LeftFoot`/`LeftHand`/`Head`, `License Accepted`) plus the bundled `public/index.html` (Aimbot/Triggerbot/ESP/Vehicle Spawn UI) identify it as a FiveM/GTA-V multiplayer cheat. server.obf.js also computes a SHA-256 fingerprint over the Windows MachineGuid (`Get-ItemProperty... Cryptography MachineGuid`), `os.hostname()`, and the C: volume serial, and POSTs `{key,hwid,nonce,app:'sensivity',version}` to a license endpoint built from a concatenated/obfuscated string. The deceptive Microsoft-OneDrive branding on the persistence artifact, the obfuscated eval'd bundle, the cross-process browser surveillance, and the auto-loaded unsigned native PE together constitute a hostile install-time payload — not a library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
